Q: A factory produces 9000 clocks a day. Every day, 3000 of them are defective as they are made incorrectly. How many clocks does the factory sell in a week?
A:

There are 7 days in a week, so the factory produces 9000 x 7 = 63000 clocks in a week. After deducting the 3000 defective clocks, the factory sells 63000 - 3000 = 60000 clocks in a week.
